title: MedicationLabelTrainingData
description: Training data schema for GoldLabel medication label OCR model. Each entry maps raw OCR text to a set of pictogram actions across 6 categories. Pictograms are language-specific images with text embedded — no separate label field. One pictogram per action per language.

raw_ocr_reference.description: The original OCR text from GCP Cloud Vision used to train the model.

medication_name.description: The extracted name of the medicine.

language.description: Language code indicating which language variant of the pictograms to use. Use 'none' for pictogram-only labels with no embedded text. The remaining 12 codes are ISO 639-1 language codes.

enumDescriptions.my: Burmese (မြန်မာဘာသာ)

pictogram_categories.description: Each property maps to a pictogram category. The value is a pictogram_id string referencing a specific action image in the database, or null if the category is not applicable for this medication.

how_to_take.description: Pictogram ID for how the medication should be taken (e.g. swallow whole, dissolve in water, take with food). 8 unique pictograms per language; 104 total across 13 languages.

side_effects.description: Pictogram ID for a notable side effect of the medication. 17 unique pictograms per language; 221 total across 13 languages.

duration.description: Pictogram ID for how long the medication course should last. 7 unique pictograms per language; 91 total across 13 languages.

dosage.description: Pictogram ID for the dose amount or frequency (e.g. 1 tablet, 2 tablets, 3 times a day). 27 unique pictograms per language; 351 total across 13 languages.

time_of_day.description: Pictogram ID for the time of day the medication should be taken. 13 unique pictograms per language; 169 total across 13 languages.

precautions.description: Pictogram ID for special precautions or warnings (e.g. avoid alcohol, avoid sunlight, keep refrigerated). 17 unique pictograms per language; 221 total across 13 languages.

examples.raw_ocr_reference: Metformin 500mg. Take 1 tablet twice daily with food. May cause nausea. Take for 30 days.
